vue.answer:
## Building an Autocomplete / Typeahead in Vue 3 **Mental model:** Autocomplete = a query + a filtered suggestion list + keyboard navigation (Up/Down/Enter/Escape). Track the text, open state, and active index as `ref`s; derive the matches with a `computed`. Roles (`combobox`/`listbox`/`option`) make it accessible. ### Step 1 — State refs ```vue ``` ### Step 2 — Derive matches with computed ```js const matches = computed(() => { const q = text.value.trim().toLowerCase(); return q ? props.items.filter((i) => i.toLowerCase().includes(q)).slice(0, 8) : []; }); ``` ### Step 3 — Keyboard navigation Use key modifiers; clamp the active index. ```vue ``` ### Step 4 — Select without losing the click Use `@mousedown.prevent` on options so selection fires before the input's blur closes the list. ```vue
  • {{ m }}
    • ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | No matches | Hide the list (or show empty row). | | Blur vs click | `@mousedown.prevent` keeps focus so the click selects. | | Async source | Debounce + cancel stale requests. | **Interview tip:** `@mousedown.prevent` on the option is the Vue-idiomatic fix for the blur-before-click race — mention it explicitly, it's the part most candidates miss.

angular.answer:
## Building an Autocomplete / Typeahead in Angular **Mental model:** Autocomplete = a query + filtered suggestions + keyboard navigation (Up/Down/Enter/Escape). Hold text, open, and active index as fields; derive matches with a getter. Classic `NgModule` setup, no `?.`/`??` (the build rejects them), no `FormsModule`. ### Step 1 — Fields ```typescript text = ''; open = false; active = -1; @Input() items: string[] = []; ``` ### Step 2 — Derive matches with a getter ```typescript get matches(): string[] { const q = this.text.trim().toLowerCase(); if (!q) { return []; } return this.items.filter((i) => i.toLowerCase().indexOf(q) !== -1).slice(0, 8); } ``` ### Step 3 — Keyboard navigation Angular key-event filters map cleanly to handlers; clamp the active index. ```html ``` ### Step 4 — Select without losing the click Use `(mousedown)` with `preventDefault` on options so selection beats the input blur. ```html
  • {{ m }}
    • ``` **Edge cases** | Case | Handling | | :-- | :-- | | No matches | Hide the list with `*ngIf`. | | Blur vs click | `mousedown` + `preventDefault` keeps focus. | | Async source | RxJS `debounceTime` + `switchMap` cancels stale requests. | **Interview tip:** Angular's `(keydown.arrowdown)` event filters keep handlers declarative; pair that with `mousedown`+`preventDefault` on options to win the blur-before-click race.
